---
title: "Lecture 27 Quiz: Cookies & Session Tracking"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-27.qmd) | [Week 9 Content](/content/week-09.qmd) | [Lab 7](/labs/lab-07.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 27 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Stateful", "B. Stateless", "C. Persistent", "D. Transactional"]
question = "Question 1: HTTP protocol behavior requires session tracking mainly because HTTP is what?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Server-side binary object only", "B. Browser-stored key-value data sent with later requests", "C. SQL row created automatically per user", "D. A JSP directive"]
question = "Question 2: Which description best matches a cookie?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Passwords in plain text", "B. Large media files", "C. Small preferences or identifiers", "D. Java class bytecode"]
question = "Question 3: Cookies are best suited for storing which kind of data?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `request.getSession()`", "B. `response.addCookie()`", "C. `session.invalidate()`", "D. `request.getCookies()`"]
question = "Question 4: Which call is used to obtain all cookies sent by the browser in the current request?"
answer = 4
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. It deletes the servlet class", "B. It clears session data and ends the current login session", "C. It converts session to cookie", "D. It forwards to login.jsp automatically"]
question = "Question 5: What is the key effect of calling `session.invalidate()` during logout?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
